key_points: Random roommates are a gamble; some horror stories reported
Dirty Dobbs dining hall is legendary but now gone
Historic dorms had underground tunnels connecting to dining
key_quotes: I had a shitty freshman year roommate, but now I live with my friends off campus
I am grateful I lived in North in 2007 to get the dirty Dobbs experience. It had character

key_points: Greek life is active but predatory religious groups target sororities and frats
Strong school spirit and rivalry culture, especially vs KU
Hacky Sack Man is a beloved campus institution — literally policy was changed for him
key_quotes: StuMo is not chill. At all. The staff never leave. Like, ever.
Sounds like a cult mixed with a ponzi scheme
A true CoMo original — Absolute LEGEND

key_points: Beloved 'Dirty Dobbs' dining hall is gone, mourned by alumni
Once it was bacon day and I ate 55 strips of bacon
MUNCH research kitchen now open to students — hidden food option
key_quotes: RIP DOBBS. I will never forget the dobbs employee that fought a bumble bee
Need me one of those Dirty Dobbs burgers right about now
Brief rant, but the burgers used fresh meat, not frozen formed patties

key_points: Student struggled with depression after aimless freshman year
Faculty describe walking on eggshells; morale is 'exceptionally low'
Tiger Pantry and Care Team resources exist for struggling students
key_quotes: Summer after freshman year I became very depressed, I felt that my time had gone in vain
I do not personally know anyone on campus who isn't walking on eggshells afraid to say anything
Everything you describe is what domestic abusers do — a chilling read

key_points: Merit scholarships are transparent and predictable based on stats
OOS students can expect ~$30k/year even after scholarships
National Merit scholars can get a full ride if Mizzou is named first choice
key_quotes: 4.0GPA, 10 APs, tri-sport — I have to pay 28k to go to Mizzou in-state
I got a $23,000 scholarship and got into the honors college — especially nice considering I'm middle class
Mizzou is fairly pricey for in-state students

key_points: Journalism school is world-class and universally respected
mucourses.com third-party tool transformed class registration for students
Group projects are pervasive and frustrating, especially senior year
key_quotes: The people that matter in journalism know Missouri is one of the very best journalism schools
MU courses changed my college experience — I've avoided so many shit classes because of it
I am in 18 credit hours and almost every class has group projects — I end up doing all the work

key_points: President Choi sent memo restricting faculty speech, causing backlash
University cancelled event for including word 'black' in the title
State legislature cut MU budget from 70% to 8% funding — hurting quality
key_quotes: He's just using academic speak for: don't say anything to make the university look bad
This is really absurd and is difficult to read as anything but anti-black
The primary culprit for MU's struggles is the Missouri State Legislature

key_points: Francis Quad and campus are genuinely beautiful, especially at night
Columbia is a solid college town with good bar specials and local spots
East campus has wildlife — coyotes and skunks spotted on commute paths
key_quotes: Francis Quad is so peaceful at night in the summer
Summers in Columbia are among my best memories of Mizzou — great drink specials at the bars downtown
I crossed paths with a skunk — my gf started freaking out but we watched bro trundle by

key_points: Journalism grads have strong alumni network and industry respect
Brad Pitt attended but left two credits shy of graduating

The people that matter in journalism know Missouri is one of the very best journalism schools
Mizzou ranked #7 on list of schools producing US senators

OOS students paying $30k+/year even after scholarships — not a bargain

Politically tense climate with admin and legislature creating friction
